senses.definition: to get or do what one wants despite the odds or other people's desires

examples.x: Despite objections from his colleagues, David had his way and implemented his innovative ideas, which turned out to be a great success.
Emily's negotiation skills were unmatched, and she always found a way to get her own way during business deals, ensuring favorable outcomes for her company.
Jane was known for having her own way in relationships, always setting her own terms and expectations, which sometimes caused conflicts but also ensured her independence and happiness.
Lily was known for her strong will and persuasive abilities, which often resulted in her getting her way during important decision-making processes.

senses.definition: used for saying that someone strongly refuses to participate in or be associated with something, often due to strong dislike, embarrassment, or a sense of moral objection

examples.x: Amy was passionate about environmental conservation and wouldn't be caught dead using single-use plastic products.
Emma is a casual dresser and would never be seen dead wearing formal attire or high heels.
Mike was a tech geek and wouldn't be seen dead using an outdated smartphone model.
She is a vegan and won't be seen dead eating meat or any animal products.

senses.definition: a type of activity, subject, etc. that one is very good at or enjoys very much

examples.x: Classical music is just not my cup of tea.
He realized that working in finance wasn’t his cup of tea.
Adventure sports have always been her cup of tea.
Teaching wasn’t his cup of tea, so he pursued engineering instead.
If horror movies aren’t your cup of tea, you might want to skip this one.

senses.definition: to be something that one is interested in
to tell someone that they are really about to enjoy something

examples.x: If you haven't tried the new restaurant in town yet, you're in for a treat.
When Sarah's parents come to visit, she knows she's in for a treat.

senses.definition: used for saying that someone can do something whenever and however they want

examples.x: The books in the library are available for checkout at their pleasure.
You may use the guest room at your pleasure.

senses.definition: a task that is done only for the sake of one's pleasure and not for any rewards of any kind

examples.x: Writing her novel was a labor of love, and she didn’t mind the long hours spent on it.
He restored the old car as a labor of love, pouring in countless hours of work.
Their efforts to renovate the house were a true labor of love, as they didn’t expect any profit.
The director worked on the film as a labor of love, creating it out of pure artistic passion.
The artist considered his mural a labor of love, creating it purely for the community's enjoyment.

word_list.pronunciation: fˈiːst wˈʌnz ˈaɪz ˌɑːn ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to observe someone or something with a great sense of pleasure or fulfillment

examples.x: The audience at the fashion show feasted their eyes on the stunning runway models, showcasing the latest designer collections.

senses.definition: something that a person finds so interesting that they cannot let go of it

examples.x: After watching one video on YouTube, I fell down the rabbit hole and spent hours watching videos on the same topic.
Jake got caught in the rabbit hole of online shopping, constantly browsing through websites and adding items to his cart, unable to resist the allure of new deals and discounts.

Lisa found herself down the rabbit hole of social media, endlessly scrolling through feeds, liking posts, and following new accounts, unable to break free from the addictive nature of online platforms.
When Sarah discovered the new TV series, she fell down the rabbit hole and binge-watched all the episodes in one sitting.

senses.definition: to perform any activity in the way that one prefers, paying no attention to what others might say or think about one

examples.x: She always does as she pleases, without worrying about others' opinions.
They do as they please, following their own path in life.
The artist does as he pleases, creating unconventional and thought-provoking artwork.
She did as she pleased during her vacation, exploring new places and trying new experiences.
In the future, she will continue to do as she pleases, living life on her own terms.
They will do as they please, unburdened by societal expectations.

senses.definition: to do whatever makes one happy or satisfied, without caring about other's opinions

examples.x: Despite the pressure to conform, she chose to do her own thing and started her own business, which became a great success.
Even though others may disapprove, he remains determined to do his own thing and pursue his passion for art.
From a young age, he had a strong independent spirit and always did his own thing, refusing to be influenced by others' opinions.

senses.definition: to turn something down because one believes that it is not satisfactory enough

examples.x: David's snobby colleagues would often turn their noses up at his ideas during meetings, dismissing them without even considering their merit.
Despite the offer of a generous salary, John turned his nose up at the job offer because it didn't align with his career goals.
Ryan will turn his nose up at any food that contains onions, no matter how delicious it may be.

senses.definition: to naturally be good at noticing, judging, or appreciating something, particularly a thing's value or a person's talents

examples.x: The decorator has an eye for color, perfectly matching shades and hues in the room.
She has an eye for interior design and can transform any space into something beautiful.

word_list.pronunciation: mˌeɪk pˈʊl ɐ fˈeɪs æt ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to alter one's facial expression in response to someone or something, often to convey emotions such as dislike, disgust, or mockery

examples.x: As the student opened his textbook to a difficult math problem, he couldn't help but make a face at the challenging equations.
She pulls a face at her brother whenever he teases her.

senses.definition: to fulfill one's need or desire for something

examples.x: Jake had a lifelong fascination with history, and he finally decided to scratch the itch by traveling to historical sites around the world.
She scratched that itch of curiosity by exploring different cultures and traditions.

word_list.pronunciation: ˌɛsbˈiː kæn kʊd tˈeɪk ɪt ɔːɹ lˈiːv ɪt

senses.definition: used to say that someone is indifferent about having, eating, seeing, or doing something

examples.x: When it comes to spicy food, Jane can take it or leave it.

senses.definition: (of an activity) too time consuming or requiring a lot of effort to be done or completed

examples.x: Cleaning the entire house by herself felt too much like hard work for Emily, so she decided to hire a professional cleaning service.
When faced with a complex problem at work, Mark felt that finding a solution would be too much like hard work, so he sought help from his colleagues.
